mechanism_narrative: Elevated federal funds rate levels increase the equity discount rate and compress valuation multiples, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns during periods with FEDFUNDS above the 12-month median are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid rise in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor income and consumer demand, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of instances when the UNRATE trigger fires.

mechanism_narrative: Joint declines in retail sales and simultaneous increases in unemployment generate a recessionary demand-labor contraction signal that amplifies downside risk and drives negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS month-over-month decline and UNRATE month-over-month increase
failure_condition: The joint signal does not produce more negative 20-day returns than either series alone (interaction term p > 0.05 in regression).

mechanism_narrative: During monetary tightening cycles, an acceleration in consumer price inflation above recent trend forces the market to price additional rate hikes, raising discount rates and producing negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL month-over-month change exceeds trailing 6-month average
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of triggered instances during tightening cycles.

mechanism_narrative: Positive surprises in retail sales reflect stronger-than-expected consumer demand, prompting immediate upward earnings revisions and positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns following positive RSAFS growth are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) compared to negative growth months.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in industrial production signals weakening manufacturing momentum and margin compression, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over a 60-trading-day horizon.
failure_condition: Negative INDPRO prints are followed by positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth surprises provide a fundamental anchor for corporate revenue growth expectations, with stronger GDPC1 prints driving positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns following high GDPC1 prints do not outperform baseline unconditional returns (Rank IC < 0.03).

mechanism_narrative: The combination of a restrictive federal funds rate above 4.5% and simultaneous contraction in industrial production creates a credit-tightening drag on corporate activity that predicts negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint condition of high FEDFUNDS and falling INDPRO does not produce significantly more negative 60-day returns than high FEDFUNDS alone (difference in means t-test p > 0.05).
